disclaimer: This is not financial advice or a guaranteed buy/sell call. Treat this as a structured watchlist and risk plan. Use your broker chart before placing orders because gap openings can invalidate these levels.

market_regime.summary: The market is weak-to-fragile, not clean bullish. Nifty closed below 24,000 at 23,897.95, Sensex fell 1.27%, crude stayed elevated, IT was under heavy selling pressure, and the rupee weakened sharply. Swing trades should be selective, smaller in size, and only near defined support/retest zones.

macro_risks.impact: Negative for India macro, inflation, current account, rupee, autos, aviation, paints, OMCs, and broad risk appetite.
macro_risks.evidence: Brent crude was reported near $107.25/barrel while the rupee logged its steepest weekly drop since 2022.

macro_risks.impact: High-valuation names can derate quickly; avoid blind breakout chasing.
macro_risks.evidence: J.P. Morgan downgraded Indian equities to neutral due to oil-led earnings risks, higher valuations, and trimmed earnings estimates across several sectors.

macro_risks.impact: Avoid fresh swing longs in large-cap IT until reversal confirmation.
macro_risks.evidence: Nifty IT lost about 10.3% for the week after weak guidance from major IT firms.

nifty_50.trade_rule: Take longs only if Nifty holds 23,800-23,900 or reclaims 24,150+. If Nifty breaks 23,600, stop forcing long swing trades.
nifty_50.evidence: Trading plan flagged caution until Nifty reclaims and sustains above 24,600, with near-term downside levels around 24,000-23,900 after a 24,100 break.

bank_nifty.trade_rule: Bank trades are allowed only if Bank Nifty holds 55,700-55,800. Fresh aggressive bank longs become better only above 57,000.
bank_nifty.evidence: Technical commentary placed Bank Nifty support near 55,700-55,800 and resistance near 56,850-57,000.

entry_split.first_entry: 60% of planned quantity inside limit-buy zone
entry_split.second_entry: 40% only after price confirms with reclaim/retest or market breadth improves

position_sizing_rules.hard_rule: Do not average down below stop-loss. That is not swing trading; that is loss denial.

enabled_strategies.name: relative_strength_pullback
enabled_strategies.definition: Stock holds green or falls less than index during market weakness, then is bought near support.

enabled_strategies.name: breakout_retest
enabled_strategies.definition: Stock near 52-week or recent swing high; buy only after breakout or controlled retest, not at random intraday spike.

enabled_strategies.name: trend_continuation_pullback
enabled_strategies.definition: Strong structural stock pulls back to previous support / demand zone while broader trend remains intact.

enabled_strategies.name: defensive_sector_rotation
enabled_strategies.definition: Money rotates into lower-beta or relatively resilient sectors during weak index sessions.

enabled_strategies.name: earnings_or_event_momentum
enabled_strategies.definition: Stock moves because of results/news; entry allowed only after the first reaction stabilizes.
enabled_strategies.warning: Never buy a huge result candle blindly. Wait for pullback or breakout continuation.

enabled_strategies.name: mean_reversion_from_support
enabled_strategies.definition: Large-cap stock near short-term support after controlled fall; buy only if support holds.

enabled_strategies.name: opening_range_breakout
enabled_strategies.definition: Wait first 30-45 minutes; buy only if stock breaks opening range high while Nifty is stable.
enabled_strategies.condition: Use only if the stock opens inside or near the planned buy zone. Do not use after a large gap-up.

enabled_strategies.name: vwap_bounce_addition
enabled_strategies.definition: Use VWAP intraday only for add-on entry, not for the first swing entry.

disabled_strategies.name: falling_knife_IT_buying
disabled_strategies.reason: IT sector has a sector-wide breakdown and weak outlook. Avoid until reversal structure appears.

disabled_strategies.name: buying_gap_up_without_retest
disabled_strategies.reason: High VIX / fragile market regime makes failed gap-ups common.

disabled_strategies.name: averaging_down
disabled_strategies.reason: Invalid for swing trading. If the stop hits, the thesis is wrong.

disabled_strategies.name: pre_result_gambling
disabled_strategies.reason: Avoid taking fresh overnight exposure just before results unless the trade is explicitly event-risk sized.

final_watchlist.why_selected: SBI closed positive while the broader market was weak. It is currently cleaner than most private bank alternatives because it showed relative strength instead of just falling less.

stop_loss.reason: Below recent intraday support and below the planned demand zone.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 30-40%. Move SL to cost for remaining quantity.
trade_management.trail_after_t2: Trail below previous day low or 20 EMA on hourly chart.
trade_management.invalid_if: Bank Nifty breaks below 55,700-55,800 or SBI closes below 1,075.

final_watchlist.why_selected: Strong close in a weak market and trading close to its 52-week high. This is a better relative-strength candidate than trying to bottom-fish broken sectors.

stop_loss.reason: Below the pullback/retest area; if this breaks, the breakout-retest thesis weakens.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book only small partial because T1 is close; main trade is T2/T3.
trade_management.invalid_if: Close below 443 or failed breakout above 462 followed by heavy selling.

final_watchlist.why_selected: High-quality liquid infra name with manageable pullback structure. Better than chasing smaller infra counters in a volatile market.

stop_loss.reason: Below the support band and below recent intraday weakness.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 30%. Hold remaining only if Nifty is above 24,150.
trade_management.invalid_if: Nifty breaks 23,600 or L&T closes below 3,915.

final_watchlist.why_selected: Still structurally strong and near highs, but volatility is higher. Good trade only if bought near support, not chased.

stop_loss.reason: Below the lower support zone. Below this, momentum structure weakens.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 40% because defence names can reverse sharply.
trade_management.invalid_if: Close below 428 or failed breakout above 456 with high-volume rejection.

final_watchlist.why_selected: Defensive large-cap setup. Not explosive, but suitable when market is fragile and IT/cyclicals are weak.

stop_loss.reason: Below nearby swing support and close enough to prevent oversized loss.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 25-30%; this is not a high-beta stock.

final_watchlist.why_selected: Strong earnings-backed momentum. Nestle reported 26% YoY profit growth and strong revenue growth, but the stock is already near a 52-week high, so entry discipline is mandatory.

stop_loss.reason: Below post-result support; below this, the result-momentum trade cools off.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book only partial because breakout above 1,431 can extend.
trade_management.invalid_if: Close below 1,362 or failed breakout above 1,432 followed by close below 1,400.

final_watchlist.why_selected: HDFC Bank is not the strongest momentum name, but it is liquid, relatively defensive, and held positive in a weak market.

stop_loss.reason: Below current base support; if this breaks, the mean-reversion trade fails.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 30% and move SL to cost.
trade_management.invalid_if: Bank Nifty breaks 55,700 or HDFC Bank closes below 766.

final_watchlist.source: Moneycontrol quote as of 24 Apr 2026 04:01; page also flagged earnings expected on 25 Apr 2026.
final_watchlist.why_selected: Technically stronger than ICICI because it is closer to its 52-week high, but upcoming earnings make it risky for overnight swing exposure.

stop_loss.reason: Below the recent low band and below the event-risk protection area.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 50% before results if already in profit.
trade_management.invalid_if: Poor result reaction, gap-down below 1,338, or Bank Nifty below 55,700.
trade_management.special_rule: Avoid fresh overnight position before result unless position size is cut by 50%.

final_watchlist.why_selected: High-quality momentum stock trading close to its 52-week high, but the stock is expensive and the day range is wide, so bad entries can hurt.

stop_loss.reason: Below the short-term support band and below recent volatility range.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book 30-40%; do not hold full size into rejection near 4,550.
trade_management.invalid_if: Close below 4,290 or broad consumer discretionary weakness.

final_watchlist.why_selected: Cipla had a wide intraday range and some supportive news/research flow, but pharma is mixed and this is not as clean as SBI/Coal/L&T. It is a conditional trade, not a first-priority trade.

stop_loss.reason: Below the intraday support zone. If 1,245 breaks, reversal structure fails.

trade_management.book_at_t1: Book only if weak rejection appears near 1,305; otherwise hold partial for 1,340.
trade_management.invalid_if: Close below 1,245 or weak pharma breadth.

highest_conviction_basket.reason: These offer the best current combination of relative strength, sector preference, liquidity, and defined invalidation.

defensive_basket.reason: Better for a fragile index environment where capital preservation matters more than aggressive upside.

conditional_basket.reason: Good only if trigger conditions are met. Do not force entry.

excluded_or_low_priority.reason: Sector-wide breakdown, weak FY27 guidance, and heavy weekly fall in Nifty IT. Avoid fresh swing longs until reversal confirmation.
Not a clean swing long right now because quarterly profit missed estimates and Middle East conflict/crude supply issues affected costs and refining earnings.
Liquid and fundamentally strong, but short-term relative strength is weaker than SBI and Axis. ICICI fell 1.62% while SBI closed positive.
High volatility and weak index structure increase slippage risk. Not worth it unless you are actively monitoring intraday.

before_market_open: Check crude oil and USD/INR direction.
first_30_minutes: Do not place full orders in first 5 minutes.
Reject any stock that gaps above avoid_chasing_above level.
If Nifty breaks below 23,800 in first hour, reduce all planned quantity by 50%.
entry_rules: Prefer limit orders inside the buy zone.
Never buy below the stop-loss just because it looks cheaper.
exit_rules: If target_1 hits, book partial profit.
If Nifty closes below 23,600, close weak trades and stop new long swing entries.
If stock hits target_2, trail aggressively instead of hoping for unlimited upside.

final_verdict.brutal_rule: The worst move now is buying 10 stocks together. Pick 3-5 max, use hard stops, and skip trades if they gap beyond the planned zone.
